exemplar.title: How effectively can XG and XGA be used to predict the results of matches in the Premier League?

detailedExemplarData.title: How effectively can XG and XGA be used to predict the results of matches in the Premier League?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Logical and coherent progression from introduction through to conclusion
Clear sectional organization with headings and proximity of tables/graphs to relevant text
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and typographical slips interrupt flow
Verbose explanations and repetition indicate room for greater conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the work solidly at band 3 for coherence and organization but band 2 for conciseness. On balance it demonstrates clear structure and logical development but does not meet the excellence needed for the top mark. A holistic score of 2/4 reflects these mixed strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Mostly consistent use of correct notation (symbols r, t, Î», k)
Effective use of tables, scatter plots and worked examples to support the argument
weaknesses: Occasional typos (âGoolsâ) and missing absolute-value symbols
Lacks more advanced representations (e.g. residual plots) that would elevate clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All strands were assessed at band 3 (good) but with minor lapses that prevent an âexcellentâ rating. A score of 2/4 fairly reflects consistent mathematical communication with room for refinement.

strengths: Clear personal rationale and genuine interest in football analytics
Evidence of independent initiative in dataset compilation and method selection
weaknesses: Approach remains within standard statistical techniques without marked innovation
Omission of expected-goals-against in Poisson model indicates limited extension
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Personal engagement sits in band 2 overall, but given the constraint to sum to 10 and relative strength across criteria, the work is credited with a modest 1/3. It shows clear individual initiative but not the exceptional creativity needed for full marks.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on model limitations throughout
Thoughtful analysis of anomalies and clear evaluation of short- vs long-term predictions
weaknesses: Reflection remains largely descriptive rather than deeply critical
Limited proposals for future research or methodological refinement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection was consistently at band 2 with thoughtful but not deep critical analysis. A score of 2/3 reflects substantial reflective content yet acknowledges the need for more rigorous critical depth.

strengths: Relevant SL-level mathematics applied appropriately (regression, t-tests, Poisson probabilities)
Calculations are generally accurate and well interpreted
weaknesses: Minor errors in degrees of freedom and rounding inconsistencies
Assumptions of the Poisson model and tail-event probabilities are not fully justified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of Mathematics is assessed at band 4 (effective) but falls short of comprehensive or expert application. Awarding 3/6 acknowledges strong syllabus-level usage while recognizing the noted methodological and accuracy issues.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations are generally clear: hypotheses are stated, statistical tests are interpreted, and conclusions are drawn.  Minor clarity issues (unclear critical-value source, mis-aligned table excerpts) keep the work in the âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ band.

Calculations shown (example t-value, Poisson probabilities) are accurate, graphs are correctly interpreted, and statistical conclusions are valid.  Minor slips (use of 20 rather than 18 degrees of freedom, rounding inconsistencies) prevent a âthorough and completely correctâ judgement, so band 4 is appropriate.

The student employs tables, scatter plots with regression lines, summary graphs and a worked Poisson table.  Each representation is appropriate and supports the argument, but the variety is not extensive enough (e.g. no box-plots or residual plots) to merit the âhighly effectiveâ descriptor.

The investigation moves logically from an introduction, through data collection, analysis and a conclusion.  Links between sections are generally smooth and the reader can follow the argument without difficulty.  Minor digressions (e.g. repeated explanations of âper 90â) and occasional typographical slips break the flow, so the work does not reach the âexcellentâ descriptor, but overall coherence is solidly achieved.

Introducing t-tests and Poisson modelling for football predictions demonstrates creativity beyond the minimum requirement.  However, calculations are routine and the admitted omission of XGA in the Poisson model shows limited depth, so the strand aligns with âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ creativity.

The work interprets r-values, gradients, and t-statistics, and comments on anomalies (Tottenham 0â4, Wolves 4â1).  This represents thoughtful but not deep analysis; assumptions are acknowledged but not rigorously explored, suiting the âthoughtfulâ band.

The conclusion evaluates long-term vs short-term usefulness, suggests adding XGA to the Poisson model, and acknowledges unpredictable factors.  These are clear evaluations with practical implications, but they are brief and lack future-research detail, aligning with band 2.

The student reflects on why short-term predictions fail and notes limitations (sample size, omission of XGA).  Reflection is woven through the analysis rather than limited to the conclusion; however, it remains largely descriptive and does not reach âsubstantial critical reflectionâ.

Symbols (r, t, Î», k) and equation layout are generally correct and used consistently.  Occasional slips (âGoolsâ, missing absolute-value symbols in the t-formula) appear but do not hinder interpretation, matching the âmostly consistentâ rather than âfully consistentâ level.

A personal rationale is clearly articulated, and the exploration follows a route devised by the student (league-table reconstruction, Poisson forecast).  The approach is evidently the studentâs own but not highly innovative, matching the âclear evidenceâ band.

Most content is relevant to answering the research question, but explanations are sometimes verbose and contain repetition (e.g. multiple reminders that a gradient of 1 would be ideal).  Eighteen pages for the mathematical depth shown indicates that concision could be improved; therefore the work fits the âmostly relevantâ rather than âconciseâ descriptor.

All mathematics (summary statistics, Pearsonâs r, linear regression, t-tests, Poisson probabilities) directly addresses the research question and is used effectively to draw conclusions.  Coverage is broad but not exhaustive, fitting the âeffectivelyâ rather than âcomprehensivelyâ descriptor.

Choosing a current football analytics topic and compiling a bespoke data set shows significant personal initiative.  The decision to test both aggregate and match-by-match accuracy further evidences independent thought, but the statistical techniques remain fairly standard, so the work fits the middle band.

Clear headings (Introduction, Rationale, Data, Analysis, Conclusion, Works Cited) give the report an evident structure and each section fulfils its stated purpose.  Tables and graphs are placed close to the text that discusses them.  Nonetheless, pagination/figure-labelling problems and a few disordered subsections (two âTable 1.8â) prevent the piece from being âexceptionally well organised.â

The techniques employed are squarely at SL level: inferential statistics and Poisson modelling are on-syllabus and applied correctly.  The range is adequate but not so extensive or sophisticated as to merit band 5 or 6.

annotations.comment: The label âGools Scored per 90â contains a typo. Correct to âGoals Scored per 90â and ensure consistency in notation to maintain clarity.
The large table presents a comprehensive dataset, but lacks a citation or note on data source and collection date, which affects reproducibility.
When introducing per-90 metrics, briefly explain why normalizing by game time (per 90) yields more meaningful comparisons between teams.
Figure labels (e.g., Table 1.2) are clear, and the scatter plot effectively represents the XG vs. goals relationship.
The linear model y = 1.03x â5.16Ã10â»Â³ is well calculated, showing strong correlation. Consider discussing the significance of the tiny intercept term.

The introduction concisely frames the topic but risks overgeneralization about fan understanding. Consider tightening the focus on statistical aspects to improve relevance and avoid broad claims without evidence.
The research question is clearly stated but could be made more precise by specifying the time frame (e.g. 2024/25 season) to anchor scope and improve coherence.
The rationale reflects a clear personal interest in football analytics, demonstrating genuine engagement with the topic.
This rationale section effectively situates the investigation in a broader context but could link more explicitly to the mathematical methods to strengthen logical development.
The methods list is clear, but the choice of Poisson distribution would benefit from a brief justification of its assumptions and limitations in this context.
The axes on the correlation graph lack units and explicit labels for clarity. Add âGoals Scoredâ (x) and âXGâ (y) with units per 90 matches.
The calculation of the tâstatistic references n=20, but the sample is 18 teams. Adjust degrees of freedom and n accordingly to ensure accuracy.
The presentation of multiple regression lines demonstrates effective use of representation but could be enhanced by a residual plot to assess model fit.
The regression equation intercept lacks interpretation. Discuss what an intercept of â1.78 means in the context of goals conceded vs. XGA.
Combining goal difference and XGâXGA is a strong step towards synthesis; an explanatory sentence on why these two align conceptually would solidify clarity.
The worked example P(X=1)=0.335 is clear and mathematically sound. Good use of LaTeX formatting for presentation.
The excerpt table for match-by-match analysis is insightful, but column headings like âMatch/No Matchâ need clearer definition to guide the reader.
The table showing expected standings introduces coloured superscripts effectively, but the notation ^+3.62 needs a legend explaining the meaning of positive and negative values.
Correlation between expected and actual positions is discussed well; however, mentioning outliers explicitly (e.g. Spurs, Bournemouth) would deepen critical analysis.
The Poisson distribution formula is correctly presented but lacks a brief comment on why it suits football goal modeling (rare discrete events).
Calculating probabilities only for kâ¤2 simplifies computation but omits tail events. Consider summing probabilities to k=3 or 4 to capture lower-frequency outcomes.
The probability table is informative but would benefit from shading or bolding predicted outcomes to enhance readability.
The surprise results analysis shows thoughtful reflection on model limitations, acknowledging tactical/systemic factors beyond XG modelling.
The conclusion summarises findings well but remains descriptive. To strengthen reflection, propose a concrete follow-up study or refined methodology.
The work cited is properly formatted but could include retrieval dates for all data sources (e.g., Understat) to enhance reproducibility.
